trivia_bullets.text: Traitorous Greed is an M21 debut — it has no prior printing and has not been reprinted since its July 2020 Core Set 2021 release as of 2026. The effect (gain control + haste + add two mana of any color) is unique among Act of Treason variants in that the mana-rider clause makes it a two-for-one: you steal their creature and fuel a follow-up play in the same turn. [Scryfall: reprint: false]

EDHREC ranks Traitorous Greed at 8,758 — significantly lower than Act of Treason variants that lack the mana bonus, which itself suggests the card's unique mana-rider clause hasn't broken through the noise of a crowded Act-of-Treason effect pool in Commander. Legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. [Scryfall legalities; EDHREC rank]

Flavor text: "Remind me again, what's the going rate for betrayal these days?" — Captain Rhenmir. Captain Rhenmir appears only on this card's flavor text and has no established presence elsewhere in published MTG lore; the name and title suggest a mercenary or pirate captain for whom betrayal is a commodity with a market price. [Scryfall]

Artist Svetlin Velinov is a Bulgarian digital illustrator who has produced over 200 Magic illustrations. He is predominantly a digital artist; of his entire catalog, only two cards have known original traditional paintings — The First Sliver (Modern Horizons), whose original sold at the MTG Art Market for $12,500 in 2019. Traitorous Greed is a digital work. [Hipsters of the Coast: 'Painting for the First Sliver Sells for $12,500', June 2019; Scryfall artist field]

related_cards.name: The First Sliver
